work_unit: SZ-PP-L-001: Base64 prefix strip regex rejects valid MIME subtypes with special characters
situation: ImageUploader.tsx line 78 uses /^data:image\/\w+;base64,/ to strip data URI prefix. \w+ matches only [a-zA-Z0-9_], rejecting valid MIME subtypes containing + or - (e.g., image/svg+xml, image/x-icon). Users uploading SVG or ICO images would get the full data URI passed as base64, causing downstream parsing failures.
action: Changed regex from /^data:image\/\w+;base64,/ to /^data:image\/[^;]+;base64,/ which matches any characters up to the semicolon delimiter, correctly handling all valid MIME subtype formats.
outcome_evidence: Commit a9aedb0 on auto/opt-popup-1783215325 pushed to origin. Regex now matches svg+xml, x-icon, and all other valid MIME subtypes. cp-guard divergence handled via case-104 direct-edit-in-wt channel.
next_suggestion: SZ-PP-L-002 (testVisionConnection no timeout) or SZ-PP-L-003 (partial storage data handling)

audit_findings.finding: Base64 prefix strip regex \w+ rejects valid MIME subtypes with special characters (+, -)
audit_findings.remediation: Replace \w+ with [^;]+ to match any chars up to semicolon delimiter
